# Implementation Plan: User Authentication (Login)

**Branch**: `001-login-auth` | **Date**: 2026-03-17 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-login-auth/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Automate user authentication (login) for the FoodTech Front application using the Page Object Model (POM) + Page Factory pattern with Serenity BDD, Cucumber, and Selenium WebDriver. The plan covers two scenarios: successful login with valid credentials (navigation to the waiter dashboard) and failed login with invalid credentials (visible error message in the DOM). The architecture enforces separation of concerns: Page Objects own locators and behavior, Step Libraries orchestrate actions, Step Definitions bind Gherkin to code, and assertions live exclusively in the step/test layer.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Java 21 (OpenJDK)  
**Build Tool**: Gradle (Wrapper preferred — `./gradlew`)  
**Framework**: Serenity BDD + Cucumber 7.x  
**Pattern**: POM + Page Factory  
**Web Driver**: Selenium WebDriver managed by Serenity  
**Reporting**: Serenity HTML reports (`net.serenity-bdd.serenity-gradle-plugin`)  
**Target Project**: AUTO_FRONT_POM_FACTORY  
**Target Application**: FoodTech Front (React 19, Vite) — running at a configurable base URL  
**Constraints**: Scenarios must be independently executable; no shared mutable state between tests; `data-testid` as primary locator strategy

### Application Under Test — Key Facts

| Aspect | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Login URL | `{base.url}/login` |
| Email/Username field | `data-testid="email-input"` |
| Password field | `data-testid="password-input"` |
| Submit button | `data-testid="submit-btn"` |
| Error message element | `data-testid="error-message"` (conditionally rendered) |
| Post-login redirect | `/mesero` (waiter dashboard) |
| Error text on invalid login | "Credenciales inválidas" |
| Valid test credentials | `test@restaurant.com` / `password123` |
| Invalid test credentials | `wrong@email.com` / `wrongpass` |

## Constitution Check (Pre-Phase 0)

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| # | Gate | §Ref | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| G1 | Scenarios validate **system behavior**, not UI mechanics | §2 | ✅ | Steps express "redirected to main operational view" and "see an error message", not "click button" |
| G2 | All scenarios are **independently executable** — no shared state | §3 | ✅ | Each scenario starts from the login page with fresh browser state; FR-007 enforces this |
| G3 | Gherkin uses Given/When/Then with **no implementation details** | §4 | ✅ | No selectors, CSS, HTTP methods in Gherkin steps |
| G4 | Serenity BDD components present: feature files, step defs, runner, config | §5 | ✅ | Plan defines all four components in the project structure |
| G5 | **POM**: Page Objects hold locators + behavior, no assertions | §6 | ✅ | LoginPage and DashboardPage hold only locators + behavior methods; assertions are in Step Libraries |
| G6 | Screenplay (AUTO_FRONT/API_SCREENPLAY) | §7 | N/A | This is a POM project |
| G7 | API project validation | §8 | N/A | This is a front-end project |
| G8 | Semantic class naming, no abbreviations | §9 | ✅ | LoginPage, DashboardPage, LoginStepDefinitions, NavigationSteps — all semantic |
| G9 | No commented code, unused variables, or unclear methods | §10 | ✅ | Plan prescribes clean code as a constraint; implementation must follow |
| G10 | Serenity report configured and generated on every test run | §11 | ✅ | `serenity-gradle-plugin` with `aggregate` task in build configuration |
| G11 | No direct implementation — spec → plan → tasks → implement flow followed | §12 | ✅ | Specification exists at spec.md; this is the plan phase |

**Result: ALL GATES PASS** — proceed to Phase 0.

```text
├── spec.md              # Feature specification (completed)

```

### Source Code (AUTO_FRONT_POM_FACTORY)

```text
AUTO_FRONT_POM_FACTORY/
├── build.gradle                          # Gradle build with Serenity + Cucumber dependencies
├── gradle.properties                     # Serenity/WebDriver configuration properties
├── serenity.conf                         # Serenity configuration (base URL, browser, timeouts)
├── features/
│   └── login/
│       └── login.feature                 # Gherkin scenarios (from spec)
└── src/
    └── test/
        ├── java/
        │   └── com/
        │       └── foodtech/
        │           └── automation/
        │               ├── pages/
        │               │   ├── LoginPage.java        # Page Object: login form locators + behavior
        │               │   └── DashboardPage.java    # Page Object: waiter dashboard locators + behavior
        │               ├── steps/
        │               │   ├── LoginSteps.java        # Step Library: login action orchestration
        │               │   └── NavigationSteps.java   # Step Library: navigation verification
        │               ├── stepdefinitions/
        │               │   └── LoginStepDefinitions.java  # Cucumber glue: Gherkin → Step Libraries
        │               └── runners/
        │                   └── LoginTestRunner.java   # Cucumber + Serenity test runner
        └── resources/
            └── serenity.conf                          # Serenity configuration (alternative location)
```

**Structure Decision**: Standard Serenity BDD + POM layout following the AUTO_FRONT_POM_FACTORY README conventions. Four-layer architecture: `pages/` (locators + behavior), `steps/` (step libraries with orchestration + assertions), `stepdefinitions/` (Cucumber glue), `runners/` (test execution entry point). Feature files live in `features/login/` at the project root, per Cucumber conventions.

### Package Naming Convention

| Package | Purpose | Constitution Ref |
|---------|---------|-----------------|
| `com.foodtech.automation.pages` | Page Objects with locators and behavior methods | §6 |
| `com.foodtech.automation.steps` | Serenity Step Libraries with orchestration and assertions | §5 |
| `com.foodtech.automation.stepdefinitions` | Cucumber step definitions (Gherkin glue) | §5 |
| `com.foodtech.automation.runners` | Cucumber + Serenity test runners | §5 |

## Architecture Design

### Layer Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Feature File (login.feature)                       │
│  Gherkin: Given / When / Then                       │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┘
                       │ binds to
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────┐
│  Step Definitions (LoginStepDefinitions.java)       │
│  @Given / @When / @Then annotations                 │
│  Delegates to Step Libraries — NO logic here        │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┘
                       │ delegates to
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────┐
│  Step Libraries (LoginSteps, NavigationSteps)       │
│  @Step annotated methods                            │
│  Orchestrates Page Object calls                     │
│  Contains assertions (shouldSee..., shouldBe...)    │
│  Serenity captures evidence per @Step               │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┘
                       │ interacts with
┌──────────────────────▼──────────────────────────────┐
│  Page Objects (LoginPage, DashboardPage)            │
│  @FindBy + PageFactory locators                     │
│  Behavior methods (enterEmail, clickSubmit, etc.)   │
│  NO assertions — returns state for steps to verify  │
│  Extends Serenity PageObject base class             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Class Responsibilities

#### LoginPage (Page Object)

- **Extends**: `net.serenitybdd.core.pages.PageObject`
- **Locator strategy**: `@FindBy(css = "[data-testid='...']")` using Page Factory
- **Elements**: email input, password input, submit button, error message element
- **Behavior methods**:
  - `enterEmail(String email)` — types into the email field
  - `enterPassword(String password)` — types into the password field
  - `clickLogin()` — clicks the submit button
  - `getErrorMessage()` — returns the text of the error element (if visible)
  - `isErrorMessageDisplayed()` — returns boolean for error element visibility
- **Constraints**: NO assertions, NO business logic (§6)

#### DashboardPage (Page Object)

- **Extends**: `net.serenitybdd.core.pages.PageObject`
- **Purpose**: Represents the main operational view (waiter dashboard) at `/mesero`
- **Behavior methods**:
  - `isDisplayed()` — verifies the dashboard page is loaded (URL or landmark element)
  - `getPageTitle()` — returns the visible page title/heading
- **Constraints**: NO assertions, NO business logic (§6)

#### LoginSteps (Step Library)

- **Annotation**: `@Steps` injected by Serenity
- **Responsibilities**:
  - `openLoginPage()` — navigates to the login URL
  - `enterCredentials(String email, String password)` — calls LoginPage methods
  - `submitLoginForm()` — calls LoginPage submit
  - `shouldSeeErrorMessage()` — asserts error message is displayed
  - `shouldBeOnLoginPage()` — asserts current URL is the login page
- **Captures**: Serenity evidence per `@Step` method

#### NavigationSteps (Step Library)

- **Annotation**: `@Steps` injected by Serenity
- **Responsibilities**:
  - `shouldBeOnDashboard()` — asserts current URL contains `/mesero` or dashboard element is visible
- **Captures**: Serenity evidence per `@Step` method

#### LoginStepDefinitions (Cucumber Glue)

- **Annotation**: Cucumber `@Given`, `@When`, `@Then`
- **Responsibilities**: Thin binding layer — translates Gherkin steps to Step Library calls
  - `@Given("the user is on the login page")` → `loginSteps.openLoginPage()`
  - `@When("the user enters valid credentials")` → `loginSteps.enterCredentials(validEmail, validPassword)`
  - `@When("the user enters invalid credentials")` → `loginSteps.enterCredentials(invalidEmail, invalidPassword)`
  - `@When("the user submits the login form")` → `loginSteps.submitLoginForm()`
  - `@Then("the user should be redirected to the main operational view")` → `navigationSteps.shouldBeOnDashboard()`
  - `@Then("the user should see an error message on the page")` → `loginSteps.shouldSeeErrorMessage()`
  - `@Then("the user should remain on the login page")` → `loginSteps.shouldBeOnLoginPage()`
- **Constraints**: NO logic, NO assertions — pure delegation

#### LoginTestRunner (Runner)

- **Annotations**: `@RunWith(CucumberWithSerenity.class)`, `@CucumberOptions`
- **Purpose**: Entry point for test execution; ties features to glue code
- **Configuration**: feature path, glue package, plugin format

## Gherkin → Implementation Mapping

| Gherkin Step | Step Definition Method | Step Library Call | Page Object Call |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Given the user is on the login page` | `givenUserIsOnLoginPage()` | `loginSteps.openLoginPage()` | `loginPage.open()` |
| `When the user enters valid credentials` | `whenUserEntersValidCredentials()` | `loginSteps.enterCredentials("test@restaurant.com", "password123")` | `loginPage.enterEmail()`, `loginPage.enterPassword()` |
| `When the user enters invalid credentials` | `whenUserEntersInvalidCredentials()` | `loginSteps.enterCredentials("wrong@email.com", "wrongpass")` | `loginPage.enterEmail()`, `loginPage.enterPassword()` |
| `And the user submits the login form` | `whenUserSubmitsLoginForm()` | `loginSteps.submitLoginForm()` | `loginPage.clickLogin()` |
| `Then the user should be redirected to the main operational view` | `thenUserShouldBeRedirected()` | `navigationSteps.shouldBeOnDashboard()` | `dashboardPage.isDisplayed()` |
| `Then the user should see an error message on the page` | `thenUserShouldSeeErrorMessage()` | `loginSteps.shouldSeeErrorMessage()` | `loginPage.isErrorMessageDisplayed()`, `loginPage.getErrorMessage()` |
| `And the user should remain on the login page` | `thenUserShouldRemainOnLoginPage()` | `loginSteps.shouldBeOnLoginPage()` | URL check via `getDriver().getCurrentUrl()` |

## Locator Strategy

| Priority | Strategy | Selector Pattern | When to Use |
|----------|----------|-----------------|-------------|
| 1 (Primary) | `data-testid` | `[data-testid='email-input']` | All elements that have `data-testid` attributes (confirmed available) |
| 2 (Fallback) | `id` | `#email` | When `data-testid` is absent but `id` is stable |
| 3 (Fallback) | `name` | `[name='email']` | When neither `data-testid` nor `id` is available |
| 4 (Last resort) | XPath | Avoided | Only if no other stable selector exists; requires justification in code review |

### Concrete Locators

| Element | Locator | Type |
|---------|---------|------|
| Email input | `[data-testid='email-input']` | CSS |
| Password input | `[data-testid='password-input']` | CSS |
| Submit button | `[data-testid='submit-btn']` | CSS |
| Error message | `[data-testid='error-message']` | CSS |
| Login form | `[data-testid='login-form']` | CSS |

## Test Data Strategy

| Data Set | Email | Password | Expected Result |
|----------|-------|----------|-----------------|
| Valid credentials | `test@restaurant.com` | `password123` | Redirect to `/mesero` |
| Invalid credentials | `wrong@email.com` | `wrongpass` | Error message: "Credenciales inválidas" |

**Rules**:
- Credentials are defined as constants in the Step Definitions or a dedicated test data class
- No shared state between scenarios — each scenario starts from a clean browser session
- No dependency on previous test execution order
- Valid credentials must be pre-provisioned in the backend (assumption from spec)
- No test data created or modified during execution

## Synchronization Strategy

| Situation | Strategy | Mechanism |
|-----------|----------|-----------|
| Page load (login page) | Wait for page readiness | Serenity `PageObject.open()` with implicit wait |
| Element interaction | Wait for visibility + interactability | Serenity `waitForVisibilityOf()` / `waitForEnabled()` methods |
| Post-login navigation | Wait for URL change | Explicit wait for URL to contain `/mesero` |
| Error message appearance | Wait for element visibility | `waitForVisibilityOf(errorMessageElement)` |

**Rules**:
- NO `Thread.sleep()` or hard waits
- Use Serenity's built-in wait mechanisms (`withTimeoutOf()`, `waitFor()`)
- Default implicit wait: configured in `serenity.conf` (recommended: 10 seconds)
- Explicit waits only when a specific condition must be met (navigation, dynamic content)

## Assertion Strategy

### Scenario 1: Successful Login

| What to Assert | How | Constitution Ref |
|----------------|-----|-----------------|
| User is redirected to dashboard | Current URL contains `/mesero` | §2 (behavior, not UI mechanics) |
| Dashboard page is loaded | Dashboard landmark element is visible | §2 |

### Scenario 2: Failed Login

| What to Assert | How | Constitution Ref |
|----------------|-----|-----------------|
| Error message is visible | `data-testid="error-message"` element is displayed | §2 |
| Error message text is correct | Element text equals "Credenciales inválidas" | §2 |
| User remains on login page | Current URL contains `/login` | §2 |

**Rules**:
- Assertions live ONLY in Step Libraries (not in Page Objects per §6)
- Use Serenity Matchers or Hamcrest for readable assertion messages
- No JavaScript alert-based validation (confirmed DOM-based in spec)

## Reporting Configuration

| Aspect | Configuration |
|--------|--------------|
| Report format | Serenity HTML aggregate reports |
| Plugin | `net.serenity-bdd.serenity-gradle-plugin` |
| Generation | `./gradlew clean test aggregate` |
| Output directory | `target/site/serenity/` (default) |
| Evidence | Serenity captures screenshots per `@Step` method automatically |
| Step naming | `@Step` annotations with descriptive text (e.g., `@Step("Open the login page")`) |
| Scenario naming | Matched to Gherkin scenario titles for traceability |

## Risks and Mitigations

| # | Risk | Impact | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|---|------|--------|------------|------------|
| R1 | Backend not running during test execution | Tests fail on login API call | Medium | Document required backend startup; consider using FoodTech's demo mode as fallback |
| R2 | Flaky timing on post-login redirect | Intermittent failures on Scenario 1 | Medium | Explicit wait for URL change with configurable timeout |
| R3 | Test data inconsistency (valid credentials not provisioned) | Scenario 1 always fails | Low | Document data prerequisites in quickstart.md; consider seed script |
| R4 | `data-testid` attributes removed or renamed | All locators break | Low | FoodTech has been stabilized for testability; monitor for changes |
| R5 | Browser driver version mismatch | WebDriver initialization fails | Low | Use Serenity's automatic driver management or WebDriverManager |
| R6 | Concurrent test execution modifies shared state | Test interference | Low | Each scenario uses independent browser session (Serenity default) |

| G9 | Clean code | §10 | ✅ | Plan prescribes no commented code, no unused variables, clear method names |
| G10 | Serenity reports on every run | §11 | ✅ | `serenity-gradle-plugin` with `aggregate` task configured |
| G11 | Spec → plan → tasks → implement flow | §12 | ✅ | spec.md → plan.md (this file) → tasks.md (next) → implement |

**Result: ALL GATES PASS** — plan is ready for Phase 1 artifacts and subsequent task generation.
